title: alpinia pusilla
intro: Alpinia pusilla, also known as the dwarf galangal or lesser alpinia, is a species of plant in the ginger family. It is native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Alpinia pusilla include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve nausea and vomiting, and reduce fever. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant are typically used, often dried and powdered, to make teas, infusions, or added to capsules for its medicinal benefits.
